mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals impending corporate revenue contraction, prompting institutional de-risking and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week of the threshold breach.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal exhibits no predictive power over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of residential construction activity and consumer credit growth indicates a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression of forward returns, or the combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over three full market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: An expansionary output gap where industrial production outpaces real GDP growth reflects supply-side efficiency gains that boost corporate operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The ratio of INDPRO to GDPC1 shows no correlation with forward equity returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or periods of supply outperformance consistently coincide with negative market returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces central bank tightening, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL breaches the threshold, or the signal generates a negative information coefficient over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous quarter-over-quarter expansion in real GDP reinforces corporate earnings growth expectations, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly investment horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if quarterly GDP growth rates show no monotonic relationship with 60-day forward equity returns, or if the signal produces a negative Sharpe ratio across multiple decades of data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong nonfarm payroll growth and a stable unemployment rate confirm a healthy labor market that sustains consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon through improved earnings visibility.
failure_condition: The joint distribution of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict forward returns better than a random baseline, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant during periods of structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid federal funds rate increases during an active tightening cycle compress equity valuations by raising the cost of capital, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon as discount rates adjust.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 5-day forward returns are positive or flat during consecutive rate hike episodes, or if the coefficient on FEDFUNDS changes sign across different tightening periods.
